docs.title: System Recovery and Reconstitution Procedures
docs.recovery_procedures: Infrastructure is rebuilt from version-controlled Terraform into a clean account, databases are restored from the most recent AWS Backup recovery point, and application services are redeployed from signed container images pinned to the last-known-good release; a runbook sequences dependencies (network, data, app, edge).
docs.reconstitution_steps: After service is restored, the system is patched to the current CM-2 baseline, host and container images are re-scanned for indicators of compromise, secrets and credentials are rotated, configuration is validated against the approved baseline, and the ISSO signs off before the system is returned to production traffic.
